posture: Inform — a clean scan: no fresh, dated NSW-REZ development cleared the relevance bar in the 24 hours to Thursday morning. The threads the radar is carrying all moved earlier in the week and had no new movement in the window: EnergyCo's Project Development Deed with Transgrid to upgrade the constrained South West REZ export path between Jerilderie and Wagga Wagga (unlocking 3.56GW for the four access-rights projects — Origin's Yanco Delta, Spark's Dinawan Energy Hub, Someva's Pottinger Energy Park and BayWa.re's Bullawah Wind Farm) was logged 15 July; Squadron's 558MW Banandra project's plan to bypass the at-capacity South West REZ and plug into the existing 330kV network was logged 14 July; and the Central-West Orana Port-to-REZ OSOM delivery campaign to Squadron's Uungula Wind Farm (underway from 13 July, ~700 loads over 12 months) plus the Main Road No. 709 gazettal continue. On New England, the Draft REZ Access Scheme Declaration remains on public exhibition (released 7 July, feedback by 6 August, targeted end-September ministerial declaration) following EnergyCo's 14 July webinars, with the EIS expected to be lodged and exhibited in the second half of 2026; the refined 1km Muswellbrook-Walcha corridor field-investigation program runs July-September (~150 affected landholders) amid live Walcha High Country Guardians opposition, the Coalition's late-June policy to scrap the 500kV corridor and cap the zone into the March 2027 election run-up, and a community-alliance disengagement threat. On Central-West Orana, ACEREZ's recommencement of access-track work under stronger heritage controls remains the latest word while the DPHI rock-shelter assessment stays open. A Central-West Orana community roadshow is flagged for 11-15 August. Publishing empty for continuity — a useful 'ran clean' signal.
summary: No new NSW-REZ-relevant item surfaced in the 24-hour window to Thursday 16 July morning. All active threads are prior-digest carry-forwards with no fresh movement — the 15 July South West REZ export-upgrade deed with Transgrid, the 14 July Squadron Banandra bypass proposal, the Central-West Orana OSOM delivery campaign and Main Road No. 709 gazettal, the New England Access Scheme exhibition (feedback by 6 August) and its 14 July webinars, the July-September field-investigation program and associated Walcha/Coalition opposition, and the ACEREZ Central-West Orana rock-shelter matter — and are deduplicated out per the writing rules. The digest is published with empty sections so the archive shows continuity.
